**Apache Flink Documentation**

> This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use the latest [stable version](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/zh/>).

#  Apache Flink Documentation #

**Apache Flink** is a framework and distributed processing engine for stateful computations over _unbounded_ and _bounded_ data streams. Flink has been designed to run in _all common cluster environments_ perform computations at _in-memory_ speed and at _any scale_.

### Try Flink

If you’re interested in playing around with Flink, try one of our tutorials:

  * [Fraud Detection with the DataStream API](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/try-flink/datastream/>)
  * [Real Time Reporting with the Table API](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/try-flink/table_api/>)
  * [Intro to PyFlink](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/dev/python/overview/>)
  * [Flink Operations Playground](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/try-flink/flink-operations-playground/>)

### Learn Flink

  * To dive in deeper, the [Hands-on Training](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/learn-flink/overview/>) includes a set of lessons and exercises that provide a step-by-step introduction to Flink.

  * The [Concepts](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/concepts/overview/>) section explains what you need to know about Flink before exploring the reference documentation.

### Get Help with Flink

If you get stuck, check out our [community support resources](<https://flink.apache.org/community.html>). In particular, Apache Flink’s user mailing list is consistently ranked as one of the most active of any Apache project, and is a great way to get help quickly.

### Explore Flink

The reference documentation covers all the details. Some starting points:

  * [DataStream API](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/dev/datastream/overview/>)
  * [Table API & SQL](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/dev/table/overview/>)
  * [Stateful Functions](<https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-statefun-docs-stable/>)

  * [Configuration](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/deployment/config/>)
  * [Rest API](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/ops/rest_api/>)
  * [CLI](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/deployment/cli/>)

### Deploy Flink

Before putting your Flink job into production, read the [Production Readiness Checklist](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/ops/production_ready/>). For an overview of possible deployment targets, see [Clusters and Deployments](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/docs/deployment/overview/>).

### Upgrade Flink

Release notes cover important changes between Flink versions. Please read them carefully if you plan to upgrade your Flink setup.

See the release notes for [Flink 1.12](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.12/>), [Flink 1.11](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.11/>), [Flink 1.10](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.10/>), [Flink 1.9](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.9/>), [Flink 1.8](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.8/>), or [Flink 1.7](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-1.13/zh/release-notes/flink-1.7/>).
